concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains several meaningful images that are missing or incorrectly rendered in the HTML output. Page 1 features a large visual collage containing maps and photographs, as well as an 'NC STATE UNIVERSITY' agency logo. The HTML output contains no image elements corresponding to either the collage or the logo. Additionally, the charts for Figure 1 and Figure 2 (page 10), and Figure 9 (page 17) are completely missing image elements in the HTML, appearing only as bracketed text placeho
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 1 contains a large collage of photos and maps, along with the 'NC STATE UNIVERSITY Aquatic Plant Management Program' logo. Page 10 contains two bar charts (Figure 1 and Figure 2).
concerns.output_quote: The HTML output is missing image elements for the page 1 collage and logo. Figures 1 and 2 are rendered only as text placeholders without `` tags, and Figures 7 and 11 are replaced by cropped footer images.
concerns.location: Page 1, Page 10 (Figures 1 and 2), Page 15 (Figure 7), Page 17 (Figure 9), Page 19 (Figure 11)

concerns.description: Table caption coded as header row: 2 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
concerns.analysis: caption_as_header=2. Examples: p8: "LAKE GASTON AQUATIC PLANT COMMUNITY - 2023"; p9: "LAKE GASTON HYDRILLA TUBER BANK ESTIMATES". First row spans all columns — should be a <caption> element, not a header row.

concerns.description: Missing images: 7 of 17 source image(s) not present in output
concerns.analysis: image_count_source=17, html_img_count=10, image_count_ratio=0.8235294117647058. Some source images are missing from the output.

concerns.description: Degraded image quality: 4 image(s) used fallback rendering instead of direct extraction
concerns.analysis: fallback_rendered_images=4. Examples: p1: "An empty white rectangle."; p1: "A solid white image."; p15: "The image displays the footer of a document page. ". These images were not directly extracted from the PDF — instead, an approximate page region was rendered as a PNG. Fallback images may be cropped incorrectly, show the wrong content, or miss meaningful visual elements.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (38): Lake Gaston Aquatic Vegetation Report 2023; 1 | P a g e; Lake Gaston Aquatic Vegetation Report 2023
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=38, pages_affected=19/20. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 2 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=0, no_th_tables=2. Tables use <th> for data values, have a data row mismarked as a header row, or lack <th> elements entirely. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 7 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (7 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p11: "...OW (Justicia americana)" showing Lake Gaston in 20"; p12: "...in 2023 showing the distribution of LYNGBYA (Micro"; p14: "...ed "HYDRILLA (Hydrilla verticillata) Lake Gaston -"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=7 (truncated=7, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

Reviewed the document for structural emphasis. While headings and bulleted lists were correctly preserved via heading and list types, the underlined emphasis on 'Prepared By:' and 'Submitted To:' on page 1 of the source PDF was lost in the JSON output, which only preserved their italics.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Underlined and italicized text: "Prepared By:" and "Submitted To:"
concerns.output_quote: Paragraphs with markdown italics but no underline: "*Prepared By:*" and "*Submitted To:*"

concerns.analysis: Matched tables have >20% row count difference (delta >=3). Pages: p9(source:6rows,output:22rows)
Matched tables have different column counts. Pages: p9(source:10cols,output:8cols)
